content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 81: The escalation rule

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to judgement and risk: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
RISK & QA | STEADY STATE | 18 min

THE BRIEF:
Define when AI assistance is not enough and a human expert is needed.

THE SITUATION:
Choose areas in your work where wrong answers matter. Create escalation rules.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Define when AI assistance is not enough and a human expert is needed. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model for situations that need legal, financial, medical, HR, technical, or leadership review.

2. Translate those into your context.

3. Write "do not use AI alone when..." rules.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- Rules are clear enough to follow under pressure.
- High-stakes domains are named.
- The rules prevent false confidence.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to privacy, bias checks, claim audits, escalation rules, red-team reviews, and any work where being wrong confidently would be more expensive than being slower carefully.

COACHING NOTE:
A good AI habit includes knowing when not to use AI. Very rude of reality, but there we are.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Save escalation rules in your operating system. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
